## Desired KIT Lifecycle [for this Release]

Acording to the [TRG 10.03 - KIT Licycle](https://eclipse-tractusx.github.io/docs/release/trg-10/trg-10-03), depending on a KIT maturity level, it will require [more or less archifacts]

- [ ] 1. Sandbox 
- [ ] 2. Incubating
  - [ ] 2.1 Draft
  - [ ] 2.2 In Progress
  - [ ] 2.3 In Review
- [ ] 4. Graduated (only for KITs which achieved the In Review State) - [see TRG 10.04](https://eclipse-tractusx.github.io/docs/release/trg-10/trg-10-04)

- [ ] 5. Deprecated (can be requested in any release) - [see TRG 10.05](https://eclipse-tractusx.github.io/docs/release/trg-10/trg-10-05)

Depending on this option, the expectations for your KIT can be determined.

## Desired KIT Category

When creating a new KIT, according to the [TRG 10.01](https://eclipse-tractusx.github.io/docs/release/trg-10/trg-10-01), KITs are identified by their Category and Domain.

**PLEASE MARK ONE OF THIS OPTIONS:**

- [ ] Dataspace Foundation

- [ ] Industry Core Foundation

- [ ] Cross Dataspace Use Case

- [ ] Industry Specific (if you marked this option, mark also which industry)
  - [ ] Automotive
  - [ ] Shop Floor
  - [ ] Semiconductor
  - [ ] Construction
  - [ ] Chemical
  - [ ] Other (may not be yet specified)


In case your industry is not specified, please inform the KIT website maintainers in the [KIT office hour](https://eclipse-tractusx.github.io/community/open-meetings#Eclipse%20Tractus-X%20KITs%20Community%20Office%20Hour).

## Desired KIT Domain

Specify to which domain your KIT belongs, example (Sustanability, Engineering, Production, Enablement Service, etc.)

## Master Data

-[] I will make sure that the my KIT latest version, creation/update date, category, domain and maturity level gets reflected in the [master data](https://eclipse-tractusx.github.io/docs/release/trg-10/trg-10-01#master-data)
